summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #501 where manual context compaction breaks chat on strict LLM servers (llama.cpp with Qwen template) by producing an orphan assistant message with no preceding user message.
summary.outcome: Fixed both compaction sites to store summaries as PersistedMessage::System instead of PersistedMessage::Assistant, updated an existing test assertion, added a regression test, and all 151 tests pass.

repo: Compaction has two separate code paths: compact() for manual /compact command and compact_session() for auto-compact on context overflow — both needed the same fix.
Strict LLM servers like llama.cpp require every assistant message to be preceded by a user message — violating this causes 'No user query found in messages' errors.
values_to_chat_messages() in crates/agents/src/model.rs converts persisted messages to chat messages; 'notice' role messages are skipped.
The moltis-chat crate has 151 tests total.

code.finding: compact() method (manual /compact) stores summary as PersistedMessage — changed from Assistant to System
compact_session() function (auto-compact on overflow) stores summary as PersistedMessage — changed from Assistant to System
Existing auto_compact test asserted role: 'assistant' — updated to 'system' to match fix
New regression test compact_session_produces_system_role added near auto_compact tests
values_to_chat_messages() handles system role correctly, passing System messages through; notice role is skipped

workflow: The fix required updating an existing test assertion that was asserting the wrong (pre-fix) behavior — a hidden form of test debt.
Running the specific new test first, then the full crate suite, is a good verification sequence.

friction: The new regression test had a compile error on first attempt, requiring an edit before the test run succeeded.
Two separate compaction sites (compact and compact_session) had the same bug independently — the duplication created risk of missing one.
open_items: PR creation was requested at the end of the transcript but not shown as completed — may still be in progress.
No cross-provider testing shown — fix correctness was only verified via unit tests, not against a live llama.cpp or other strict server.
